summary: Cachebox migration is fully implemented. All alru_cache/async_lru usages replaced with cachebox equivalents across 30+ files. All a_sync cache_type='memory' decorators have ram_cache_maxsize bounds set. New y/_cache.py module provides centralized cache factories. Comprehensive tests (15 cachebox tests) all pass. Dependencies correctly updated in requirements.txt and pyproject.toml.

nonBlocking.description: y/_cache.py has 5 mypy type errors: LRUCache and TTLCache missing generic type parameters (e.g., cachebox.LRUCache[Any, Any]). Project uses strict mypy but mypy exits 0 due to pre-existing errors codebase-wide.
nonBlocking.suggestedFix: Add type parameters: cachebox.LRUCache[Any, Any] and cachebox.TTLCache[Any, Any]

nonBlocking.description: test_uniswap_v1 3 ERROR fixtures (async_uni_v1 not found) are pre-existing upstream test issues unrelated to cachebox migration.
nonBlocking.suggestedFix: Pre-existing issue. No action required for this milestone.

fullSuite.notes: 834 failures and 3 errors are consistent with pre-existing concurrent test infrastructure failures documented in baseline-green.md. No new failures attributable to cachebox migration.

sharedStateObservations.description: cachebox LRUCache and TTLCache require generic type parameters for strict mypy: use cachebox.LRUCache[Any, Any] instead of cachebox.LRUCache in type annotations.
sharedStateObservations.content: When using cachebox with strict mypy, always specify generic type parameters: cachebox.LRUCache[Any, Any] and cachebox.TTLCache[Any, Any] to avoid 'Missing type parameters for generic type' errors.
